markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify the CRDT scope does not conflict with the deferred realtime multi-user UI boundary. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: CrdtSpecCompatibilityCheck leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CrdtSpecCompatibilityCheck is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 bffa7b64; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: EXERCISES MUSTs AGAINST REAL IMPL + REAL DB, not constants. must_authority_changes_flow_through_event_ledger_promotion (knowledge_crdt_proof_tests.rs:520-580): drafts -> asserts fact_count==0, then attempts a DIRECT INSERT into knowledge_crdt_promoted_facts with fabricated 'fabricated-event-1/2' promotion ids and asserts it FAILS on the FK to kernel_event_ledger. This is a genuine negative proof of 'direct authority write outside the promotion path is invalid'. must_every_edit_class_leaves_typed_receipts (587-690): real push asserts actor+state-vector+ledger receipts, then a spanless graph proposal ('"not-an-array"'::jsonb) FAILS the span CHECK and an anonymous AI edit ('operator:not-a-model') FAILS the model-actor regex. must_denial_receipts_are_durable_and_ledger_paired (697-718): an anonymous/ledger-less denial INSERT FAILS. These are real fail-closed DB negatives, not tautologies.

MIXED TAUTOLOGY (note): must_postgres_event_ledger_is_the_only_crdt_authority (724-772) has BOTH real and tautological parts. Real: it sets a record's storage_authority=FileSystemAuthority + update_bytes_ref='file://...' and asserts validate_crdt_update_record rejects BOTH fields -- a genuine validator negative. Tautological: it also asserts the contract object's denied_authority_refs/denied_durable_surfaces *contain* certain strings, which only proves the constructor matches itself. The deferred-realtime test (781-807) is real: validate_yjs_update_envelope passes a full envelope then rejects it after session_id is blanked, proving identification cannot be dropped.

GAP vs the spec MUST set: MT-080 enumerates (1) promotion-only authority, (2) typed receipts per edit class, (3) durable ledger-paired denials, (4) Postgres-only authority, (5) deferred-UI identification. It does NOT test the spec MUST that 'KnowledgeClaim/edge MUST carry EVIDENCE SPANS that actually exist'. Because the promoted_facts span CHECK is non-empty-array ONLY (no FK to knowledge_spans), MT-080 cannot and does not catch the MT-069 dangling-evidence hole -- in fact the suite's own MT-077 e2e promotes a 'pending:' span as authority. So the spec-compatibility check is INCOMPLETE on the evidence-span MUST.

migration rollback (the prompt's 0150-0155 question): verified separately from this test -- each down-migration DROPs its table(s) + DELETEs registry rows; headers document FK reverse-order ('roll back 0153 first'); DROP without CASCADE preserves FK integrity (out-of-order rollback fails loudly rather than silently orphaning). Leaves zero objects when run in correct reverse order. Not exercised by an automated test here, but the down SQL is correct by inspection.
evasion_probes: Probed whether MT-080 would catch a non-existent span becoming authority: NO -- there is no test asserting span existence, and the promoted_facts schema permits arbitrary span strings. The MUST 'claims carry evidence spans' is checked only for NON-EMPTINESS, not existence.
Probed whether the 'direct authority write invalid' MUST is real: YES -- the fabricated-event FK-rejection INSERT is a true negative test.
Probed whether identification can be dropped for single-user UI: NO -- blanked session_id fails envelope validation.
Probed the storage-authority MUST: filesystem authority posture + file:// ref are rejected by validate_crdt_update_record on both fields (real).
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0153_knowledge_crdt_promoted_facts.sql:33-36 (span CHECK is non-empty-array only -- the gap MT-080 cannot catch)
Rerun status: test rerun deferred (tree mid-foreign-WIP, knowledge_code_index wired into lib.rs:180); static review of committed test source stands. POSTGRES_TEST_URL not set in review shell.

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (33 CRDT tests pass under runtime-full,test-utils).

evidence: Hardened d6adf2dd: Spec-compat gains the evidence-span-existence MUST negative test.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.
evidence_basis: Subagent and local WP Validator review of only the MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION at session read.
Direct packet status/dependency map checked before mutation.

Targeted local cargo probes passed for MT-054, MT-056, and MT-063 hardening paths: relationship_id_resists_separator_injection_collision; db_blocks_retired_to_accepted_resurrection_via_raw_sql; scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply.
MT-049..MT-064 were independently confirmed by the returned subagent slice using real PostgreSQL-backed test paths.
wp_validator_review_v1.residual_uncertainty: Validation was scoped to MTs already READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Concurrent WIP/untracked files outside those MTs were not inspected.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
